surface_verification.justification: Compliance/traceability INFRASTRUCTURE change (backfill engine worktree-scan fix + config-aware TT5 enforcement gate + 189 @FR tag data). No web/UI/API surface and nothing the WebUI renders field-for-field is touched; spec_impact none (no product FR behaviour change), so the E2E/cross-layer gates do not fire. Verified via the Python suites (shared/tests 4170, backfill 239, gate suites 29, adopt/test/iterate/compliance plugins, integration-tests 232) + ruff.

behaviors.behavior: engine scans a repo nested under a prune-named ancestor (the .worktrees worktree)
in-tree node_modules / __pycache__ still pruned after the prune-predicate change
behaviors.evidence: same test asserts neither node_modules nor __pycache__ appears

behaviors.behavior: enforcing gate sees config-opted plugin-dir coverage (base predates config)
gate misses plugin coverage without config (load-bearing control)
union floor: a config narrowing below defaults cannot false-green the removal gate
monotonic re-scan: head dropping the config cannot false-green the removal gate
rename-target re-scan: a test moved to a NEW dir + FR removed + config dropped cannot false-green
exclude_dirs load-bearing: a fixture-path test cannot fake a required layer
189 @FR tags written correctly; a plugin-dir tag round-trips into the manifest with 0 orphans
covers pytest marker registered in iterate/test/adopt pyprojects so tagged tests don't warn
behaviors.reason: pyproject config verified by collecting the tagged suites (all green, no strict-marker error)
